conj.statement: All geographic tiers cite *timeliness* of data as critical for effective intervention (NCSL, 2024; NGA Center, 2023; NASBE, 2022). 2. **Equity focus**: Rural, suburban, and urban districts prioritize intersectional data collection to address hidden gaps (Urban Institute, 2024; AASA, 2023). 3. **Infrastructure solutions**: High-speed broadband partnerships (e.g., rural FCC RDOF, urban Google Fiber) are universally recommended (FCC, 2024).North Carolina’s state assessment mandates conflict with CMS’s desire for localized SEL metrics (NCDPI vs. CMS Equity Office, 2024). 2. **Data privacy vs. util
supporting_evidence: Community assets**: United Way of Central Carolinas funds *Family Data Literacy Nights*, reaching 5,000 parents annually (United Way, 2024).
contradicting_evidence: Infrastructure gaps**: 31% of rural districts lack sufficient bandwidth for real-time data dashboards (ADE, 2024, p. 28).
Capacity constraints**: Only 37% of rural teachers report confidence in using data to adjust instruction (ADE, 2024, p. 45).
Equity misalignment**: Data systems do not consistently capture social-emotional or attendance metrics for early intervention, contributing to persistent achievement gaps (ADE, 2024, p. 52).
Infrastructure strain**: 22% of schools lack high-speed broadband, hindering real-time data access (FCC Broadband Report, 2024).
Workforce capacity**: 63% of paraprofessionals and 41% of teachers require data literacy training (CMS PD Needs Assessment, 2024).
Equity misalignment**: Data systems do not consistently capture intersectional identities (e.g., Black male students with disabilities), obscuring nuanced achievement gaps (CMS Equity Office, 2024).
County variations**: Urban (timeliness, intersectionality), Suburban (resource allocation), Rural (infrastructure, workforce capacity).
conj.subtopic: Assessment and Data Use for Continuous Improvement
conj.state_variations: Alaska** (remote/rural infrastructure gaps); Hawaii** (island-specific logistics); Arkansas** (rural bandwidth inequities); Mississippi** (poverty-driven data gaps); Wyoming** (sparse population challenges); County variations**: Urban (timeliness, intersectionality), Suburban (resource allocation),
conj.county_variations: Contradictions resolved**: State mandates vs. local needs (via waivers for SEL data), privacy vs. utility (via anonymized data pools), sustainability (blended funding models).
